revision.comment: Adding [[Category:11]][[ja:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:11]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷}} * {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Official positions in Japan|ja|官位}} * {{ill|Japanese court ranks|ja|位階}} * {{ill|Reimei Doyashiro|ja|歴名土代}}
== External Links == * Digital Collections of the National Diet Library: ''Kugyō Bunin'' [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991099 Part 1], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991100 Part 2], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991101 Part 3] * [http://bunin.kiin.net/ Kugyō Bunin Quick Access] (A summarized list divided into 13 periods from 923 (Engi 1) to 1052 (Eishō 7)) * {{Kotobank}}

revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:Removing this excess]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷}} * {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Official positions in Japan|ja|官位}} * {{ill|Japanese court ranks|ja|位階}} * {{ill|Reimei Doyashiro|ja|歴名土代}}
== External Links == * Digital Collections of the National Diet Library: ''Kugyō Bunin'' [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991099 Part 1], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991100 Part 2], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991101 Part 3] * [http://bunin.kiin.net/ Kugyō Bunin Quick Access] (A summarized list divided into 13 periods from 923 (Engi 1) to 1052 (Eishō 7)) * {{Kotobank}}

'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:qq-" to "[[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--"
revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷}} * {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Official positions in Japan|ja|官位}} * {{ill|Japanese court ranks|ja|位階}} * {{ill|Reimei Doyashiro|ja|歴名土代}}
== External Links == * Digital Collections of the National Diet Library: ''Kugyō Bunin'' [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991099 Part 1], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991100 Part 2], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991101 Part 3] * [http://bunin.kiin.net/ Kugyō Bunin Quick Access] (A summarized list divided into 13 periods from 923 (Engi 1) to 1052 (Eishō 7)) * {{Kotobank}}

'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. '''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷}} * {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Official positions in Japan|ja|官位}} * {{ill|Japanese court ranks|ja|位階}} * {{ill|Reimei Doyashiro|ja|歴名土代}}

The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].

revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.

Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. revision.comment: Text replacement - "冠位・位階制度の変遷}}" to "冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}}"
revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}} * {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Official positions in Japan|ja|官位}} * {{ill|Japanese court ranks|ja|位階}} * {{ill|Reimei Doyashiro|ja|歴名土代}}

Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.

'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (8qq),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614

'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (8qq),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (8qq),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}} * {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Official positions in Japan|ja|官位|1=Kan'i system}} * {{ill|Japanese court ranks|ja|位階}} * {{ill|Reimei Doyashiro|ja|歴名土代}}

However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (8qq),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (公卿補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (8qq),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}} * {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Official positions in Japan|ja|官位|1=Kan'i system}} * {{ill|Japanese court ranks|ja|位階}} * {{ill|Reimei Doyashiro|ja|歴名土代}}

revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (Kugyō補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}} * {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制}} * {{ill|Official positions in Japan|ja|官位|1=Kan'i system}} * {{ill|Japanese court ranks|ja|位階}} * {{ill|Reimei Doyashiro|ja|歴名土代}}
== External Links == * Digital Collections of the National Diet Library: ''Kugyō Bunin'' [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991099 Part 1], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991100 Part 2], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991101 Part 3] * [http://bunin.kiin.net/ Kugyō Bunin Quick Access] (A summarized list divided into 13 periods from 923 (Engi 1) to 1052 (Eishō 7)) * {{Kotobank}}

'''Kugyō Bunin''' (Kugyō補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Bump-01-18]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (Kugyō補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />

The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.

'''Kugyō Bunin''' (Kugyō補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. '''Kugyō Bunin''' (Kugyō補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}} * {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system}} * {{ill|Official positions in Japan|ja|官位|1=Kan'i system}} * {{ill|Japanese court ranks|ja|位階}} * {{ill|Reimei Doyashiro|ja|歴名土代}}

revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (Kugyō補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Tier 2]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (Kugyō補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks}} * {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system}} * {{ill|Official positions in Japan|ja|官位|1=Kan'i system}} * {{ill|Japanese court ranks|ja|位階}} * {{ill|Reimei Doyashiro|ja|歴名土代}}
== External Links == * Digital Collections of the National Diet Library: ''Kugyō Bunin'' [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991099 Part 1], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991100 Part 2], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991101 Part 3] * [http://bunin.kiin.net/ Kugyō Bunin Quick Access] (A summarized list divided into 13 periods from 923 (Engi 1) to 1052 (Eishō 7)) * {{Kotobank}}

'''Kugyō Bunin''' (Kugyō補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-ranking officials in the [[Imperial Court of Japan]], serving as a fundamental [[historical source]] for the study of [[Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding ranks equivalent to [[senior third rank]] or higher, such as [[Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], [[Naidaijin|Minister of the Interior]], [[Dainagon|Major Counselor]], [[Chūnagon|Middle Counselor]], [[Sangi|Councilor]], and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to the first year of the [[Meiji era]].
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to [[Shōryaku]] era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the [[Chōwa]] era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early [[Heian period]].<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|ja|類聚国史}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|ja|日本紀略}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to [[Kanpyō]] eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
