revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kugyō Bunin]][[fa:کوگیو بونین]][[ja:公卿補任]][[zh:公卿補任]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kugyō Bunin]][[fa:کوگیو بونین]][[ja:公卿補任]][[zh:公卿補任]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kugyō Bunin]][[fa:کوگیو بونین]][[ja:公卿補任]][[zh:公卿補任]]
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (Kugyō補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-Ranking officials in the {{ill|Imperial Court in Kyoto|en|Imperial Court in Kyoto|WD=Q11680900|lt=Imperial Court in Kyoto|1=Imperial Court in Kyoto}}, serving as a fundamental {{ill|Historical source|en|Historical source|WD=Q3750478|lt=Historical source|1=Historical source}} for the study of [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding Ranks equivalent to [[senior third Rank]] or higher, such as [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[:en:Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], {{ill|Naidaijin|en|Naidaijin|WD=Q1185338|lt=Minister of the Interior|1=Naidaijin}}, {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Major Counselor|1=Dainagon}}, {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Middle Counselor|1=Chūnagon}}, {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Councilor|1=Sangi (Japan)}}, and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Taikei|1=Kokushi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10926780}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} to the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}.
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to {{ill|Shōryaku|en|Shōryaku|WD=Q1324858|lt=Shōryaku|1=Shōryaku}} era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the {{ill|Chōwa|en|Chōwa|WD=Q1194173|lt=Chōwa|1=Chōwa}} era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Rikkokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|en|Ruijū Kokushi|WD=Q1038639|lt=Ruijū Kokushi|1=Ruijū Kokushi}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to {{ill|Kanpyō|en|Kanpyō|WD=Q6364393|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō}} eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchida Naoshige|1=Naoshige Tsuchida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423817}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Taikei|1=Kokushi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10926780}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614
== Notes == === Annotations === <references group="notes" /> === References === <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Changes in the System of Ranks and Titles|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}} * {{ill|Government of Japan|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}} * {{ill|Official positions in Japan|ja|官位|1=East Asian government service ranking|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Official positions in Japan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452076}} * {{ill|Japanese court Ranks|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese court Ranks|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Court Ranks in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} * {{ill|Reimei Doyashiro|ja|歴名土代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reimei Doyashiro|1=Ryakumyō Dodai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546895}}
== External Links == * Digital Collections of the National Diet Library: ''Kugyō Bunin'' [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991099 Part 1], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991100 Part 2], [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991101 Part 3] * [http://bunin.kiin.net/ Kugyō Bunin Quick Access] (A summarized list divided into 13 periods from 923 (Engi 1) to 1052 (Eishō 7)) * {{Kotobank}}

It lists the names of individuals holding Ranks equivalent to [[senior third Rank]] or higher, such as [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[:en:Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], {{ill|Naidaijin|en|Naidaijin|WD=Q1185338|lt=Minister of the Interior|1=Naidaijin}}, {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Major Counselor|1=Dainagon}}, {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Middle Counselor|1=Chūnagon}}, {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Councilor|1=Sangi (Japan)}}, and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Taikei|1=Kokushi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10926780}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} to the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}.
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to {{ill|Shōryaku|en|Shōryaku|WD=Q1324858|lt=Shōryaku|1=Shōryaku}} era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the {{ill|Chōwa|en|Chōwa|WD=Q1194173|lt=Chōwa|1=Chōwa}} era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Rikkokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|en|Ruijū Kokushi|WD=Q1038639|lt=Ruijū Kokushi|1=Ruijū Kokushi}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to {{ill|Kanpyō|en|Kanpyō|WD=Q6364393|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō}} eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchida Naoshige|1=Naoshige Tsuchida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423817}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Taikei|1=Kokushi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10926780}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kugyō Bunin]][[fa:کوگیو بونین]][[ja:公卿補任]][[zh:公卿補任]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kugyō Bunin]][[fa:کوگیو بونین]][[ja:公卿補任]][[zh:公卿補任]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kugyō Bunin]][[fa:کوگیو بونین]][[ja:公卿補任]][[zh:公卿補任]]
'''Kugyō Bunin''' (Kugyō補任) is a personnel record listing the names of high-Ranking officials in the {{ill|Imperial Court in Kyoto|en|Imperial Court in Kyoto|qid=Q11680900|lt=Imperial Court in Kyoto|1=Imperial Court in Kyoto}}, serving as a fundamental {{ill|Historical source|en|Historical source|qid=Q3750478|lt=Historical source|1=Historical source}} for the study of [[:en:Japanese history|Japanese history]].
== Overview == The work records the official positions of the Imperial Court year by year. It lists the names of individuals holding Ranks equivalent to [[senior third Rank]] or higher, such as [[:en:Daijō Daijin|Chancellor]], [[Sesshō and Kanpaku|Regent and Chief Advisor]], [[:en:Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]], [[:en:Udaijin|Minister of the Right]], {{ill|Naidaijin|en|Naidaijin|qid=Q1185338|lt=Minister of the Interior|1=Naidaijin}}, {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Major Counselor|1=Dainagon}}, {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Middle Counselor|1=Chūnagon}}, {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|qid=Q1133663|lt=Councilor|1=Sangi (Japan)}}, and non-councilor officials. The names are listed in order of their positions, with family names abbreviated. For instance, the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|qid=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}} appears as "Fuji○○" instead of "Fujiwara ○○." Each name is followed by additional details, such as birth and death years, promotions, and appointments. The version in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Taikei|1=Kokushi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10926780}} series covers the period from the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} to the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}.
The author and date of compilation of Kugyō Bunin remain unknown. However, it is believed to have been based on the "Rekiunki" (歴運記) compiled in [[:en:Kōnin|Kōnin]] 2 (811),<ref group="notes">Also referred to as "Kōnin Rekiunki." Although quoted in works like the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, the complete text is lost.</ref> with subsequent additions made for later periods. The final form of the work, with detailed annotations, is thought to have taken shape during the [[Ōwa (Heian period)|Ōwa]] to {{ill|Shōryaku|en|Shōryaku|qid=Q1324858|lt=Shōryaku|1=Shōryaku}} era (late 10th century). The section for non-councilor officials seems to have been supplemented after the {{ill|Chōwa|en|Chōwa|qid=Q1194173|lt=Chōwa|1=Chōwa}} era.
Although it serves as the most comprehensive source on official appointments, inaccuracies exist, particularly in entries concerning the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.<ref group="notes">Despite the availability of more reliable contemporary records such as the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|qid=Q1151168|lt=Rikkokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|en|Ruijū Kokushi|qid=Q1038639|lt=Ruijū Kokushi|1=Ruijū Kokushi}}, and the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|qid=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}, discrepancies in Kugyō Bunin's entries for the Heian period (Kōnin to {{ill|Kanpyō|en|Kanpyō|qid=Q6364393|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō}} eras) have been noted. Since {{ill|Tsuchida Naoshige|ja|土田直鎮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchida Naoshige|1=Naoshige Tsuchida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11423817}}'s study on its compilation history, citations from Kugyō Bunin for this period are approached with caution.</ref> Revisions, including retroactive adjustments, are thought to have been made for political reasons, leading to occasional discrepancies with actual appointments.
== Compilations == Kugyō Bunin is included in the {{ill|Kokushi Taikei|ja|国史大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Taikei|1=Kokushi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10926780}} series: * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 53: Kugyō Bunin Part 1'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003568 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 54: Kugyō Bunin Part 2'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003582 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 56: Kugyō Bunin Part 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003599 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 57: Kugyō Bunin Part 5'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 978-4642003605 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Supplement: Kugyō Bunin Index'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee. ISBN 4642003614

ISBN 978-4642003575 * ''Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei Vol. 55: Kugyō Bunin Part 3'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, edited by Kuroita Katsumi and Kokushi Taikei Editorial Committee.
